data.body:
# Plan of record: LLM code review for rebar via Gerrit gate + three-pass review structure

## Decisions locked
- Architecture: adopt Gerrit as the gatekeeper for code merged to rebar `main`; self-host Gerrit + rebar MCP together on AWS; GitHub as a downstream read-only mirror (Go-project topology), with GitHub `main` locked so only Gerrit's replication identity can update it. LLM review integrates as a Gerrit `LLM-Review` label gated by a submit requirement; `submitWholeTopic` lands whole reviewed features atomically. This replaced an earlier custom content-digest "review provenance" design, which the research showed reinvents what branch-protection / merge gates / Gerrit already solve.
- Review structure (ALL rebar reviews): three passes. (1) first pass emits {finding, criteria[] in locked rubric, evidence[] flexible free-text, scenarios[], impact}; (2) a SEPARATE verifier AGENT re-grounds in code and emits severity attributes {prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} + typed BINARY verification sub-answers; (3) a DETERMINISTIC aggregator computes severity (from attributes) and validity/confidence (single conditional veto + graded fraction of binary answers) and decides block/advisory against per-criteria thresholds (project-set, start high -> mostly advisory). No model-emitted holistic severity or confidence anywhere in the decision path.
- Confidence = binary decomposition (Chain-of-Verification / FactScore / SAFE / RAGAS-faithfulness / TICK pattern), aggregated deterministically. The only veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a code citation is present (avoids veto brittleness; supports non-citable absence findings). "is-verifiable" demoted from veto to graded signal (too subjective for a veto).
- Methodology/rubric: Google eng-practices + Anthropic security taxonomy (locked, versioned) + PR-Agent confidence/abstention discipline; Conventional Comments dropped. rebar's native finding contract kept; severity populated deterministically, not model-emitted.
- Out of scope for these epics: interactive escalation (autonomous process), threshold calibration (start high, gather dogfood data).
- Hosting: AWS is the most expensive option (~$50-70/mo); Hetzner ~$18/mo is the value leader; GerritHub free for OSS. Co-hosting Gerrit + rebar MCP on one ~8 GB box is sound (pin Gerrit heap ~2 GB, watch git GC IO spikes, no external DB via NoteDb).

## Epics created
- Infra prerequisite: d251-05e1-eb55-43b2 (AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP; GitHub main locked to Gerrit).
- Reviewer migration / three-pass framework: 9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b.
- Agentic code-review capability (off-by-default, source-separated; dogfood on rebar): b744-4fb9-2d05-4b49. Blocked by both above.
- Comment added to plan-review epic 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa (adopt the three-pass standard).
- Bug filed: 94a3-8bb2-5d4a-442b (tracker .gitignore omits write-lock files).

## Research basis (~14 web research spikes)
git review-provenance prior art; LLM review engines (PR-Agent/Qodo, CodeRabbit, etc.); GitHub/GitLab/Gerrit gating + merge automation (bors, merge queues, Zuul); stacked-diffs / integration-branch graduation (git seen/next/master, linux-next); Gerrit-as-gatekeeper usage (Go GerritBot, OpenStack/Zuul, Chromium CQ, Android, Qt); LLM-on-Gerrit prior art (amarula / official ai-code-review plugins); Gerrit hosting cost; severity/blocking/confidence handling across tools + 2026 research; binary-decomposed confidence (CoVe, FactScore, SAFE, RAGAS, DeepEval, TICK).
